response.justification: The candidate has strong distributed systems and geospatial domain knowledge plus proven engineering fundamentals, but falls short on primary technical requirements. C++ experience ended in 2014-2015 and is not modern, with no Rust experience at all. Additionally, there is no background in navigation or routing systems. For a mid-senior IC role requiring productive contribution with modern C++, these gaps are substantial and difficult to overcome quickly.
strengths: Extensive geospatial/GIS domain expertise as GISCollective founder and through Green Map work, directly relevant to routing tile data
Proven ability to build high-performance distributed systems at scale (Dunnhumby: millions of HTTP requests/second with low-latency requirements)
Strong software engineering discipline with demonstrated commitment to testing, TDD, and code quality across all roles
concerns: C++ experience is severely dated (last used 2014-2015, 10+ years ago) and insufficient for role requiring modern C++ development
No Rust experience despite it being listed as an acceptable alternative primary language
No background in navigation guidance systems, routing algorithms, tile generation, or map optimization—critical domain knowledge for this role

key_factors.factor: Modern C++ or Rust proficiency gap
